title: Confused over Dialogue
wikitext:
[]: Confused over Dialogue.             Quote Post by The Goose » Wed Feb 28, 2007 2:52 pm In EOE, when the Egg of Lilith appears, Futuyuki says, "But even that depends on the will of Lilith." I'm confused with what hes trying to say here. He knows that Lilith's soul is in Rei and that Gendo controls it, so why does he think that Lilith, herself, will choose what will happen with 3I instead of Gendo?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Feb 28, 2007 4:46 pm Fuyutsiki's dialogue throughout the entirety of End of Evangelion is probably critical to unlocking whatever is going on. So as you can imagine, we are still largely clueless as to what he's on about.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Feb 28, 2007 5:07 pm BB's translation: ep26' BB script wrote: Fuyutsuki (off screen): The egg of Lilith... the genesis of human life... the Black Moon... We have no desire to return to that empty shell, (on screen) But even that depends on the will of Lilith. Gendo: It seems to have begun... Rei, take me to Yui's side.

[]: Re: Confused over Dialogue.             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Feb 28, 2007 5:19 pm The Goose wrote: In EOE, when the Egg of Lilith appears, Futuyuki says, "But even that depends on the will of Lilith." I'm confused with what hes trying to say here. He knows that Lilith's soul is in Rei and that Gendo controls it, so why does he think that Lilith, herself, will choose what will happen with 3I instead of Gendo?

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Thu Mar 01, 2007 6:25 am I'm also confused over another bit of dialouge. When all the souls are coming back to earth, this is heard: Rei: Anyone can return to human form as long as they can imagine themselves in their own heart. (The Eva series go silent and fall to Earth... Crosses rise into the heavens) Yui (voice): Don't worry. All living things have the ability to return to their original form... and the heart to go on living. Anywhere can be heaven as long as you have the will to live. After all, you're alive... and you can find the chance to achieve happiness anywhere.-End of Evaneglion Why does the film say that humans can come back two times? Its not like viewers were deaf from what Rei said. Also Ornette Coleman, when Futusuyki says his line, the films cuts to Lilith on the cross, not Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Mar 01, 2007 6:55 am The Goose wrote: I'm also confused over another bit of dialouge. When all the souls are coming back to earth, this is heard: Rei: Anyone can return to human form as long as they can imagine themselves in their own heart. (The Eva series go silent and fall to Earth... Crosses rise into the heavens) Yui (voice): Don't worry. All living things have the ability to return to their original form... and the heart to go on living. Anywhere can be heaven as long as you have the will to live. After all, you're alive... and you can find the chance to achieve happiness anywhere.-End of Evaneglion Why does the film say that humans can come back two times? Its not like viewers were deaf from what Rei said. Also Ornette Coleman, when Futusuyki says his line, the films cuts to Lilith on the cross, not Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Thu Mar 01, 2007 9:45 am I know they were reassuring him,l but why do they say humans can come back two times. And after Fuyutsuki's line, they have a close up of Lilith. Plus, Rei didn't reject Gendo because of his wish to take into one. She rejected him to go and help Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Mar 01, 2007 9:56 am The Goose wrote: I know they were reassuring him,l but why do they say humans can come back two times.

[]:             Quote Post by The Goose » Thu Mar 01, 2007 2:58 pm Then again, Fuyutsuki's line doesn't matter even if Rei doesn't have Lilith's soul. After all, they have LLilith captured in Central Dogma, so Gendo has control over that too. Also: Shinji: Kaworu said that he loved me. It was... It was the first time someone told me they loved me. He was like me, and like Ayanami. I loved him. Kaworu was the one who should have survived. He was a much better person than I am. Kaworu should have been the one to survive.-from Episode 24 What does Shinji mean that Kaworu was like Ayanami and him?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Mar 02, 2007 12:39 am The Goose wrote: Then again, Fuyutsuki's line doesn't matter even if Rei doesn't have Lilith's soul .
